bead.title: Refresh balanced heimq benchmark baseline
bead.description:
After the diagnostic benchmark and fetch-path optimization beads land, rerun the heimq baseline and update the benchmark documentation with balanced evidence. The purpose is not to hide produce performance; it is to report the remaining produce/consume gap with enough context to tell client-side polling overhead from broker fetch overhead.

Required documentation:
- Current commit SHA benchmarked.
- Produce throughput and consume throughput for the default baseline.
- Diagnostic consume output, including consumer poll count and records per non-empty poll.
- Any tuned consumer-fetch profile numbers added by the diagnostic bead.
- A short explanation of remaining asymmetry and any follow-up bead needed.

In-scope files:
- crates/heimq/benches/BASELINE.md
- crates/heimq/tests/bench_baseline.rs only if benchmark output formatting needs small cleanup

Out-of-scope:
- New broker optimizations.
- Niflheim benchmark updates.
- Changing historical comparison tables without rerunning the matching command.
bead.acceptance:
1. `BENCH_RECORDS=100000 BENCH_LATENCY_SAMPLES=2000 BENCH_DIAGNOSTICS=1 cargo test -p heimq --release --test bench_baseline -- --ignored --nocapture` passes.
2. crates/heimq/benches/BASELINE.md records the new commit SHA, default produce/consume numbers, diagnostic consume stats, and any tuned-fetch profile numbers.
3. `cargo test -p heimq --test bench_baseline -- --list` passes.
